title: stellaria laevis var. typica
intro: Stellaria laevis var. typica, commonly known as the lesser celandine, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help soothe wounds, reduce swelling, and combat infections. The health benefits of Stellaria laevis var. typica include its potential to alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to support digestive health. Herbal preparations of Stellaria laevis var. typica, such as infusions and tinctures, can be made by steeping the plant's roots or leaves in hot water or a solvent like ethanol, allowing for the extraction of its medicinal compounds.
